name: Volatility 3 (Memory Forensics Framework)
pricing_notes: Open source (Volatility Software License); Volatility Foundation

auth.notes: Local tool operating on memory dumps; no auth

performance.notes: Memory image analysis speed varies by plugin and dump size

agent_readiness.notes: Industry-standard memory forensics framework. python vol.py -f memory.dmp windows.pslist. Plugins: pslist (process list), pstree, cmdline (command lines), netscan (network connections), dlllist, filescan, handles, malfind (malware detection), yarascan. Windows, Linux, macOS. Symbol tables for OS-specific analysis. Plugin framework (Python). Used by incident responders, malware analysts, threat hunters. v3 complete rewrite with Python 3 and improved architecture.

gotchas: Requires correct symbol tables for target OS/version
Memory dumps must be acquired with proper tools (winpmem, LiME)
Steep learning curve — requires memory forensics knowledge
False positives in malfind require manual analysis
best_when: DFIR: memory forensics during incident response
Malware analysis: extracting artifacts from infected memory dumps
Threat hunting: finding hidden processes, injected DLLs
CTF competitions with memory forensics challenges
avoid_when: Live system analysis (use EDR tools instead)
summary: Volatility 3 is the industry-standard Python memory forensics framework. vol.py -f memory.dmp windows.pslist/pstree/cmdline/netscan/malfind. Windows/Linux/macOS analysis. Used by incident responders and malware analysts for extracting artifacts (processes, network connections, DLLs, injected code) from memory dumps. v3 complete Python 3 rewrite.
